title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions, which has sparked both excitement and curiosity within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI, a company specializing in asset financing. This deal marks ReOrbit's expansion in the commercial satellite market.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract to supply a satellite bus for a Japanese technology demonstration mission. This deal marks Apex's expansion into the Japanese space market.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract with the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting U.S. defense and hypersonic technology development.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 25 commercial players
summary: Space Command is hosting a classified wargame involving 25 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations between Space Command and the commercial sector.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: AI could enable cyberattacks to hijack satellites, potentially causing collisions and a cascade of debris that makes Earth orbit unsafe within two years. The industry faces urgent questions about preparedness for such threats.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit today
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' today, March 19. The launch can be watched live.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a free $50 voucher for a two-year subscription, coinciding with the release of two previously 'missing' Doctor Who episodes on BBC iPlayer.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is set to roll out the Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of its planned April 1 launch. The event will be broadcast live for public viewing.

title: 'At the edge of what we thought possible': Astronomers find extremely rare star from ancient universe
summary: Astronomers discovered an extremely rare, iron-deficient second-generation star from the ancient universe, offering insights into how early stars enriched subsequent stellar generations.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Australia's Central Australia experienced significant rainfall in early 2026, turning its desert landscape green. This unusual weather event marked a stark contrast to the region's typically arid conditions.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: The Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission is led by the University of New Brunswick, Canada, with planning and operations conducted by the APXS team.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon, the Philippines' most active volcano, on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows with infrared data. PHIVOLCS reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds during this period.
company: Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS)

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from military service to a NASA Pathways engineering internship at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in engineering environments through the program.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The news lists various upcoming space industry events in 2026, including gatherings organized by organizations like MSBR, SIA, Ansys, and NASA, as well as policy-focused conferences and leadership luncheons.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship program ahead of Flight 12. This brings the next Starship launch one step closer to readiness.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine missions planned, including flights by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum. The launches are expected to take place from various global spaceports.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin has introduced a new mission concept called NEO Hunter, aimed at planetary defense. The hybrid system is designed to detect and potentially deflect near-Earth objects (NEOs).

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its goals of sending humans to the Moon and Mars at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to Starship development.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: A new crew arrived at the International Space Station (ISS) in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in January. The month was marked by new crew arrivals and scientific activities aboard the ISS.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 years of its hallmark achievements in space exploration and technology. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions over the past five decades.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, mandating its suppliers to use the new costing software for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission next year. This deal marks Apex's entry into the Japanese space market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: United Launch Alliance is accelerating modifications to the Vulcan rocket, including improvements to the nozzle and solid rocket booster, to prepare for a summer launch. The changes were planned before a recent anomaly, but are now being prioritized.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round. The company aims to reduce global reliance on satellite infrastructure.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another step in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a new proposal with the FCC stating that its SkyTerra Next plan will not cause interference. The proposal involves hosting an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites within Ligado's existing L-band coordination.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly supply solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea's sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) national security constellation. Hanwha Systems is also involved in the project.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article highlights the critical importance of satellite communication (Satcom) terminal survivability in contested environments for maintaining command and control, mission execution, and safety-of-life communications. It emphasizes the reliance of U.S. military platforms on beyond-line-of-sight connectivity in denied areas.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic announced plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' Earth observation satellites, which will offer one-meter resolution and daily mapping capabilities. The company aims to enhance its Earth observation services with this new constellation.

title: 9 New Faces for SATShow Week 2026
summary: SATShow Week 2026 introduces nine new speakers, expanding its focus on military space with the inclusion of GovMilSpace for its second year. The event gathers key figures in the satellite industry.
